name: Performance Efficiency
description: How efficiently the system uses time, memory, and capacity for the work it does. Time behaviour, resource utilisation, and capacity headroom keep response times steady under expected and peak load.

requirements.text: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)

refs.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

requirements.text: Sequential awaits on independent async calls MUST be parallelised

refs.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available

requirements.text: Synchronous blocking I/O MUST NOT be used in async contexts

refs.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available

requirements.text: String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern

refs.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

requirements.text: Regular expressions MUST NOT use catastrophic backtracking patterns

refs.name: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity
Regular expressions MUST NOT use patterns susceptible to catastrophic backtracking

requirements.text: Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results

refs.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

requirements.text: Queries on large tables MUST use indexes and MUST NOT perform full table scans

refs.name: Excessive Data Query Operations in a Large Data Table
Queries on large tables MUST use indexes and MUST NOT perform full table scans

requirements.text: Repeated lookups MUST use indexed structures (maps, sets) instead of linear search

refs.name: Excessive Execution of Sequential Searches of Data Resource
Repeated lookups MUST use indexed structures (maps, sets) instead of linear search

requirements.text: CPU computation MUST NOT be unnecessarily inefficient

refs.name: Inefficient CPU Computation

requirements.text: Unbounded result sets MUST be paginated or limited

refs.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

requirements.text: Database connections MUST use connection pooling

refs.name: Data Resource Access without Use of Connection Pooling

requirements.text: Large collections MUST use lazy iteration (generators, streams) instead of full materialization

refs.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

requirements.text: Caches MUST have eviction policies; unbounded caches are forbidden

refs.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

requirements.text: File and network handles MUST be released promptly after use

refs.name: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
Resources MUST be released promptly after use

requirements.text: Operations MUST NOT allow small inputs to trigger disproportionate resource consumption

refs.name: Asymmetric Resource Consumption (Amplification)
Operations MUST NOT allow small inputs to trigger disproportionate resource consumption

requirements.text: Resource allocation MUST be throttled with configurable limits

refs.name: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
Resource allocation MUST be throttled with configurable limits

requirements.text: Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible

refs.name: Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop
Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible

requirements.text: Mutable default arguments MUST NOT be used; create fresh instances per call

refs.name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component

requirements.text: Memory allocation MUST NOT use excessively large size values from untrusted input

refs.name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value

requirements.text: Batch operations MUST be preferred over individual operations for bulk data

refs.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

requirements.text: Background tasks MUST be offloaded from request-handling threads

refs.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available

requirements.text: Memory-intensive operations MUST use streaming instead of loading entire datasets

refs.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

requirements.text: Thread/coroutine pools MUST have bounded sizes with configurable limits

refs.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

requirements.text: Recursive algorithms MUST have depth limits or use iterative alternatives

refs.name: Uncontrolled Recursion
Recursive algorithms MUST have depth limits to prevent stack overflow
